feed.title: SAP Community - SAP BTP, ABAP environment
feed.subtitle: SAP BTP, ABAP environment blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Minimum Pay/Wage concept in SuccessFactors Time Tracking
entry.content:
Background:

Some organizations that I have come across have a minimum wage setup. For instance, if an employee has a 8 hour work schedule Mon – Fri, they get a minimum pay equivalent to working 4 hours even if they spend 5 minutes working for that day. And further beyond 4 hours, they get the regular pay for the number of hours they work in that day.

 

How can we set this up in Time Valuation?

You might already have some of your own ways to achieve this. I found a new way of dealing with this with a new time valuation method which was introduced in 2311 i.e using Create Time Record Method. Hence, I thought of sharing the same if it helps you in any way.

Let me illustrate how this can be done in a simpler way.

 

The below is a time recording profile (Clock Times) that I am going to use to achieve this.

The first three evaluations are very self explanatory and most of this you would already know. They get recorded working time and deduct any unpaid breaks from them. Hence I would not get into details of them. Essentially the starting point would be recorded times without breaks.

 

Now the main business starts from 4th evaluation until the last one

Create a dummy 4 hour record as a sample

It can have any start time and end time as fixed value as long the duration is 4 hour

In the above example, I have it from 6:00 AM to 10:00 AM

Check Min wage/pay 4 Hours

In this time valuation we will check if recorded times excluding breaks is below the 4 hour threshold or not. It uses a compare threshold with comparison group and route input type.

If net recorded working times is less than 4 , we route the input (which is also net recorded times) to Time type group below where we store hours recorded below 4 hours for further minimum wage/pay check. If recorded hours are more than 4, then we move them to Time type group above where we pay the regular wage for the employee as per the hours worked for that day. The TTG Above CLT-PAY-REG is set as time pay type for payroll

 

Pay Min 4 Hours If Required

In this final time evaluation, we actually pay the minimum pay of 4 hours if the employee has worked less than 4 hours for that day. This too usesa compare threshold with comparison group and route input type.

As you can see , we compare the results of previous time valuation (where we stored the hours below 4 hours threshold in TTG) as a comparison group and we compare that with the threshold 0 hours.

If CLT- Store Below 4 hours is > 0, it indicates that employee has minimum 1 minute of recorded time for that day. In that case we route the input time type group Min 4 HR Record or dummy 4 HR record to Time type group above CLT – Final pay min wage where employee is paid a minimum wage or pay equivalent of 4 hours for that day.

 

Now let’s test this out

Testing

 

The employee works for just 1 hour for a day

But still gets 4 hours paid out. The minimum wage/pay concept has triggered here

Next, the employee works for 6 hours in a day. He/She gets standard pay for 6 hours as seen below

This is one of the simpler ways of achieving this requirement which may sound a bit complex to begin with.

Hope the new dimension of “CREATE TIME RECORD” time valuation is unearthed here and you use it in your time valuations

Would be happy to hear about your feedback

Thanks

Neelesh

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear David,

Up to now I work as SAP functional consultant for more than 10 years, major in SD module. I would like to get certification in SAP S4 HANA - Sales and Distribution to improve my CV & support my work.

I hope you will consider my application for the free certification. I'm a women & single parent also.

Many thanks & regards,

entry.title: Re: Areeba's Journey to Learn SAP Skills: Shattering Glass Ceilings in Pakistan
entry.content: You are an inspiration for lots of girls. Keep it up.

entry.title: Farewell to an Era: The End of an Iconic Certification of SAP BODS - C_DS_42
entry.content:
Hello All,

 

continuing to the previous blogs on SAP DS 4.2 and 4.3

  1. End of ERA - SAP BODS 4.2
  2. SAP BODS / Data services road map DS 4.3 | SAP Blogs
  3. SAP Data services (BODS) 4.3 release / Installation / new features of BODS 4.3 | SAP Blogs

Today, we're diving into a topic near and dear to the hearts of many in the data services realm: the legendary certification, C_DS_42, for BODS 4.2. For over a decade, this certification has been the holy grail for data services consultants, a badge of honor symbolizing expertise and proficiency in SAP's Data Services platform.

However, as with all good things, its time has come to an end. On the 28th of March 2024, the C_DS_42 certification officially expired, marking the end of an era for many professionals who have cherished and pursued it.

What makes this transition even more surprising is the fact that its successor, C_DS_43, the certification for the latest version of Data Services, has also met the same fate, entering the realm of expired certifications.

For those who have dedicated countless hours to mastering BODS 4.2 and preparing for the C_DS_42 exam, this news may come as a bittersweet moment. It's a reminder of the ever-evolving nature of technology and the necessity of staying current in a field where innovation waits for no one.

But fear not, for every ending marks a new beginning. While bidding farewell to C_DS_42 may evoke a sense of nostalgia, it also presents an opportunity for renewal and growth. As the industry moves forward, so must we.

So what's next? For those who held C_DS_42 with pride, it's time to embark on a new journey. Whether it's upgrading to the latest version of Data Services or exploring other avenues within the data management landscape, there are plenty of opportunities to expand our horizons and continue making a meaningful impact.

As we reflect on the legacy of C_DS_42, let's remember the skills and knowledge it has imparted upon us. Let's honor the hard work and dedication that went into achieving this certification, and let's carry that same passion into the next chapter of our professional lives.

Farewell, C_DS_42. You may be expired, but your legacy lives on in the countless professionals you've inspired along the way.

Here's to new beginnings and the endless possibilities that lie ahead.

Thanks,

Venkatesh Golla

entry.title: Re: Farewell to an Era: The End of an Iconic Certification of SAP BODS - C_DS_42
entry.content:
1. End of ERA - SAP BODS 4.2
  2. SAP BODS / Data services road map DS 4.3 | SAP Blogs
  3. SAP Data services (BODS) 4.3 release / Installation / new features of BODS 4.3 | SAP Blogs

entry.title: Re: Provision users from Microsoft Azure AD to SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication
entry.content:
Hello Harjeet,

is it advisable or practical to synchronize users from Azure Active Directory (AD) to SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) in a simple installation setup, such as:

Azure - IAS - SAP BAS or SAP SAC.

Before making this decision, I'm eager to hear recommendations from SAP or experts in the field.

Are there any best practices or specific scenarios where synchronizing users between Azure AD and SAP IAS is particularly beneficial?

Conversely, are there any reasons why this might not be advisable?

Many thanks


Best regards

entry.title: Re: Certify Your Skills as SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Consultant
entry.content:
Dear Dieter,

I read your blog post and all the comments.

I am appearing for C_S4CPB_2402 certification (Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) in 2 weeks. I have completed my course learning "Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Consultants". As mentioned in your comments, I have now started course "Exploring SAP Cloud ERP". 

I am still not confident about the exams and answering the questions adequately. Anything else that you suggest covering - any mock exams, any other course etc.

Any guidance would be helpful. 

Thanks

Amit

entry.title: Re: Stock Type Determination of Inbound Delivery in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM)
entry.content: Thank you @saurabh_nigade - Your inputs to this blog are an icing on the cake.

entry.title: SAP Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, Private Cloud is here!
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the release of Feature Package 01 for SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management 2023. This release demonstrates our continued commitment to empowering businesses to improve their supply chain execution and run their transportation operations efficiently.

Key features in this release include:

  1. Integration innovations:
    1. Sales order scheduling – a much anticipated innovation that allows a customer service representative to use data from Transportation Management when determining the delivery date for the sales order. For more details see this blog.
    2. MM Scheduling Agreement – this innovation allows a more selective pull of schedule lines into TM resulting in performance improvements since only relevant schedule lines are integrated. For more details see this blog.
    3. Incoterms support - for certain incoterms (e.g., EXW and FCA) changes were made to ensures ASNs are sent when the transfer of risk occurs.
    4. Advanced handling when warehouse loading stops are not completed. This process helps planners manage the exception process including the unassignment of cargo that was not loaded and the re-assignment of the cargo for later pickup.
    5. Event based customer invoice - it is now possible to automatically trigger the customer invoice when a defined event has posted reducing manual efforts.
    6. The left behind at the port process simplifies the process of re-assigning containers to another booking when they are not loaded on the ship as planned, simplifying the exception process for transportation planners.
  2. Planning & optimization:
    1. Routing can now be configured to include or exclude routes with tolls and routing quality is improved with the addition of a report to identify any triangle inequalities.
    2. Drive time and working constraints such as rests are considered by planning and scheduling and are visible in the Gantt chart. As a result, more accurate and executable trips that follow relevant driver safety regulations can be created.
    3. When creating a package unit, the packaging material can be selected at the same time, simplifying the process and improving usability for transportation planners.
  3. Collaboration innovations:
    1. The procurement process was improved so carriers can submit multiple bids in a single auction while also seeing the current lowest rates.  On the shipper side, for those managing RFQ’s it is now easier to create and monitor RFQs: multiple carriers can now be mass-inserted in a single step and RFQ’s are automatically updated upon receipt of confirmations.
    2. Carriers can now submit deviating time window information (revised ETA’s) via the collaboration apps which will update the freight order details which are then available to other parts of the organization (e.g., customer service).
  4. Other innovations:
    1. Improvements to the freight order interface enable sending and receiving freight orders with attachments, allowing shippers and carriers to share documents relevant to a specific freight order. 
    2. In the settlement area, there is now the ability to configure the settlement process that allows changes to freight orders after the accruals process and invoices are posted.  This simplifies how changes are shared between TM and financial accounting.

For further details on the innovations included in SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management 2023 FPS1 refer to the What’s New Blog.

This feature pack (FPS1) is the first release in the new cadence of feature packs being released every six-months between the major releases. The next scheduled feature pack for the 2023 release is scheduled for Q4 of 2024.

At SAP, we are dedicated to driving innovations and delivering solutions that empower businesses to thrive in today’s dynamic supply chain environment. To learn more about SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Transportation Management private edition, please contact your SAP Account Executive.

entry.title: Re: Insights from the Pregame Show for the Next Generation SAP Enterprise Architect Forum 2024
entry.content: Nice work Paul and Shaun, giving back to the Architect community is awesome, and focusing on the next generation can only benefit the client base.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano, I also would like to participate in this program. I consider myself as pet below:

  • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
  • First generation college students
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I am an economic imigrant which tries to develop a career in the new country. SAP skillset is very in demand therfore I trust it will open the doors to the new career.

Best regards

Maciej

entry.title: Re: First steps using the Hana Vector Engine with SAP GEN AI
entry.content:
Hey!!! amazing blog!!! I can create the DB; the embeddings but when execute the embeed step I have this error. 

 

alueError                                Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[13], line 25
     23     content = p
     24     model_dim = 'ADA_1536'   ## OPENAI ADA ... Dimensions 1536
---> 25     embedding =  get_sap_genai_ada_embedding(content)
     27     csv_rows.append([i,content,model_dim,embedding])
     29 ### insert csv rows into Embedding Content

Cell In[10], line 2, in get_sap_genai_ada_embedding(input)
      1 def get_sap_genai_ada_embedding(input) -> str:
----> 2   response = embeddings.create(
      3       input=input,
      4       model_name="text-embedding-ada-002",
      5       #encoding_format='base64'
      6   )
      7   return str(response.data[0].embedding)

File ~/anaconda3/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gen_ai_hub/proxy/native/openai/clients.py:88, in Embeddings.create(self, input, model, deployment_id, model_name, config_id, config_name, **kwargs)
     81 model_name = if_set(model_name, if_set(model))
     82 model_identification = kwargs_if_set(
     83     deployment_id=deployment_id,
     84     model_name=model_name,
     85     config_id=config_id,
     86     config_name=config_name,
     87 )
---> 88 deployment = proxy_client.select_deployment(**model_identification)
     89 model_name = deployment.model_name or '???'
     90 with set_deployment(deployment):

File ~/anaconda3/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gen_ai_hub/proxy/core/utils.py:68, in lru_cache_extended.<locals>.decorator.<locals>.wrapped_func(_recache, *args, **kwargs)
     66     objs[id(obj)] = obj
     67 try:
---> 68     ret = cached_method(cache_key, *args, **kwargs)
     69 finally:
     70     if first_arg_self:

File ~/anaconda3/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gen_ai_hub/proxy/core/utils.py:58, in lru_cache_extended.<locals>.decorator.<locals>.cached_method(cache_key, *args, **kwargs)
     55     id_, _ = cache_key
     56     args = (objs.pop(id_),) + (args or tuple([]))
---> 58 return func(*args, **kwargs)

File ~/anaconda3/lib/python3.10/site-packages/gen_ai_hub/proxy/gen_ai_hub_proxy/client.py:182, in GenAIHubProxyClient.select_deployment(self, raise_on_multiple, **search_key_value)
    180 except IndexError:
    181     pass
--> 182 raise ValueError('No deployment found with: '
    183                  + ', '.join([f'deployment.{k} == {v}' for k, v in search_key_value.items()]))

ValueError: No deployment found with: deployment.model_name == text-embedding-ada-002


Do you have any idea about this?? I

entry.title: BKP – Simplify your user input with proper variant setup via configuration activities
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Customer Returns Solution (Scope Item BKP) can handle different returns scenarios in an effective way.  The system pre-defined process variants and corresponding options empower you a variable and flexible end to end process scenarios.

By default, the process variants and their options are all activated as a maximum capability to consume. In your real customer returns business, you might just have certain returns processes which require a subset of process variant options. To simplify your user input, and improve user experience, you could utilize the available configuration activity to customize your business solutions.

Logistical follow up activity - Customer Returns (BKP Scope Item) offers a list of typical logistical follow up activities for you to consume and accelerate the logistical processing – corresponding follow up document would be auto triggered once released. 

  • Configuration activity 105526 Activate and Rename Follow-Up Activities

With this configuration, you can just activate and rename the necessary follow up activities according to your real business.

By default, all follow up activities are activated, you can deactivate some follow up activities which do not fit your real business.

For example,

  • You can de-activate 0005 and 0007 if in your customer returns, you never forward the returned product to your supplier.
  • You can de-activate 0008 and 0013 if you never have product inspection or keep the product at your customer site.
  • You can de-activate 0026 and 0027 if you don’t have in-house repair business with customer returns.

With proper activation of only needed follow up activities, you would see less options in follow up activities in returns item creation, or product inspection.  This makes it easier for your end users and ensure a precise process.

 

Refund Control - Customer Returns (BKP Scope Item) offers several refund control options to specify how and when the refund is to be processed.

Configuration activity 105716 Activate and Rename Refund Control

With this configuration, you can just activate and rename the necessary refund control according to your real business.

For example,

  • According to your refund policy, if you never have an immediate refund to customer, you can de-activate the option I Immediately.
  • Meanwhile, you can customize your meaningful description based on the two different refund types.

 

Note – for above two configuration activities, you had better de-activate the values that are not needed at the project beginning phase. If later you want to de-activate a specific value, system would check any returns order has already used the specific value.

  • For 2SL system, once a specific value is used in production system, you can’t de-activate it.
  • For 3SL system, once a specific value is used in test or production system, you can’t de-activate it.

 

Configuration activity 106585 Define Default Values for Customer Returns Apps

You could use this configuration activity to accelerate the user input in apps ‘Manage Customer Returns -Version 2’ and ‘Create Customer Return Without Reference - VA01 with default values’.

The returns process variant values can be defaulted at plant level.

With above default values, when you input the material number into the returns order item, system would set the default values for your configured variants.

You could utilize this configuration activity to reflect your main returns scenario.

 

With above configuration activities, you can improve your experience with the apps by simplifying the input.

 

Related Documents

Customer Returns at help portal

Customer Returns (BKP)

entry.title: Re: Addressing Common Questions and Complaints About the New Platform

entry.title: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: It's a wrap!
entry.content:
Yesterday, we concluded March's developer challenge in the SAP Community - All about CloudEvents and event-driven architectures. Thanks to everyone who took part in it :-). I hope you had as much fun participating in the challenge as I had preparing it.
 
In case you missed the developer challenge but you are interested in knowing what it was all about... you can get started here: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 1.
If you want to learn more about CloudEvents and event-driven integrations, I would recommend keeping an eye on the content of the Event-driven integration CodeJam - https://github.com/SAP-samples/events-driven-integrations-codejam, which is now close to being finalised :-). Also, remember that you can request/host an SAP CodeJam event in your city or company, and we will be happy to help you run it - https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/so-you-want-to-host-a-codejam-everything-you-need-to-know/ba-p/221415.
 
Now, some cool stats on community members' participation:
  • A total of 36 SAP Community members took part in the challenge.
  • Unique SAP community members participation per week:
    WeekTotal submissions
    Week 131
    Week 226
    Week 323
    Week 416
    Total96
  • Total submissions per week. Note: In week 2 a couple of participants submitted more than one solution and in weeks 3 and 4, I'm counting as valid submissions any valid message received. Many participants submitted more than one message.
    WeekTotal submissions
    Week 131
    Week 228
    Week 370
    Week 498
    Total227
  • Last but certainly not least... a special mention to all the SAP Community members who completed the challenge every single week 🎉
    @Alpesa1990
    @MatLakaemper
    @MioYasuatke
    @Nagarajan-K
    @PriyankaChak 
    @Ruthiel 
    @ajos
    @emiliocampo
    @geek
    @pamoli_banerjee
    @r00k13d3v
    @saitgunacorel
    @spirit2681
    @xavisanse

If you are interested in the event-driven integrations/architectures topic.... 📺 stay tuned as there is way more coming later this year in this space.

entry.title: Re: How to Manage Customer Experience with Data & Analytics

entry.title: Re: First steps using the Hana Vector Engine with SAP GEN AI
entry.content:
Thanks for the positive feedback.  I'm pleased you're are giving it a try.

From the errors it looks like in SAP AI CORE (needs to be the extended plan) you may not have configured and deployed the foundation model 'text-embedding-ada-002'  yet.

The configuration should look something like:

Once you've created the config you then need to deploy and check its running:

This may help  models-and-scenarios-in-generative-ai-hub 

 

Alternatively if you don't have the AI CORE Extended plan you can use public API's like those from OpenAI to perform the Embeddings. Check this out: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/embeddings/what-are-embeddings

Good luck with your continued tests.
